Ὀλοφέρνης, ου, ὁ Holofernes, Assyrian commander-in-chief, slain by Judith in his own tent when he laid siege to her native city of Bethulia (Jdth 2ff) 1 Cl 55:5.—On the name and pers. of H. s. JMarquart, Philol. 54, 1895, 507ff; Pauly-W. VIII 1167–71; Schürer III/1, 216–18.

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

pers. pers. = person(s)

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

Philol Philol , Zeitschrift für das klassische Altertum—List 6

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 704
